metadata.description: Automated-researcher skills: remote GPU jobs, experiment lifecycle support, and adversarial claim verification. Extracted from a working autonomous-research lab.

plugins.name: gpu-job
plugins.description: Give your coding agent GPU powers: disposable cloud GPU pods with your environment restored, detached job execution, artifact persistence, verified teardown (never bill an idle GPU). Backend: RunPod.

plugins.name: verify-claims
plugins.description: Verification gates that catch the confidently-wrong-number class before money or conclusions move: deterministic full-pool data audits plus adversarial fact/design/data/evidence checks read by an independent model family (read-only sandboxed).

plugins.name: experiment-lifecycle
plugins.description: Run GPU experiments like a researcher — the full lifecycle in three skills: design-experiment (write a DESIGN.md data-collection spec, clear it with the human through cross-family validity gates, emit a START.md brief + CHECKLIST.md, dispatch to a fresh-context executor), log-experiment (land the design-stage pre-registration, and later the finished result, to the research repo as a gated PR), and run-experiment (the zero-context executor: self-wake, acquire/provision/drive/collect/close). Instance specifics live in an execution profile; pairs with gpu-job and verify-claims.

plugins.name: feedback-loop
plugins.description: Keep the scaffold improving while agents use it: file-feedback routes product friction to the configured product Issue tracker or drafts deployment-only notes through instance guidance, and triage-feedback maintains dispositions and routes product fixes through ship-change when configured.
